prompt: Build a REST API using Express with Postgres and deploy to AWS ECS
assertions: Phase 1 (DISCOVER) executes with at least one Socratic clarifying question
Technique D challenges 'Express' as a constraint-or-choice
Technique D challenges 'Postgres' as a constraint-or-choice
Technique D challenges 'AWS ECS' as a constraint-or-choice
At least 2 of the 3 named specifics (Express, Postgres, AWS ECS) are challenged via Technique D
Technique B (alternative framings) fires at least once during Phase 1
Technique B presents exactly 3 framings: a current frame, an alternative frame, and a reductive frame
Technique B's option 3 (reductive frame) is qualitatively different from options 1 and 2 — not just 'a smaller version of frame 1'
Phase 3.5 (RESEARCH) executes with at least one WebSearch invocation
Phase 3.5 surfaces at least one managed/serverless alternative such as Supabase, Render, Railway, Fly.io, Neon, Vercel, or PlanetScale
At least one of the surfaced alternatives is evaluated against the chunk's constraints (functionality match, cost, lock-in, etc.)
Each evaluated candidate is assigned a classification (Adopt fully / Adopt partially / Reject / Inspire) with a specific reason — not a vague 'doesn't fit' rejection
